summary: The reproduction bundle demonstrates SSRF with auth token leakage via the ML backend URL. An authenticated user registers an attacker-controlled URL as an ML backend, and Label Studio sends the project creator's auth token to that URL in the setup() POST body. Docker-based execution was not completed due to network constraints (Docker image pull timeout in the execution environment).
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause Label Studio to create an ML backend pointing at a local HTTP listener, triggering a setup() POST request that contains the project creator's auth token (access_token field) in the request body delivered to the attacker-controlled URL.
actual_observed_result: Not attempted. Docker image pull timed out in the execution environment due to network constraints. The source code was statically verified: label_studio/ml/api_connector.py:232-243 confirms that setup() sends project.created_by.auth_token.key in the POST body to the user-controlled ML backend URL.
verification_method: The exploit.py script starts a local HTTP listener on port 9999, creates a Label Studio user and project, registers the listener URL as an ML backend via POST /api/ml, and checks whether the captured POST body contains the project creator's auth token. Success is determined by string-matching the token in the captured request body.
evidence_produced_by_run: Static code review confirmed label_studio/ml/api_connector.py:239 sends access_token: project.created_by.auth_token.key to the ML backend URL
Static code review confirmed label_studio/ml/serializers.py:34-36 calls validate_upload_url with block_local_urls=settings.ML_BLOCK_LOCAL_IP which defaults to False
Static code review confirmed label_studio/core/settings/base.py:733 sets ML_BLOCK_LOCAL_IP = get_bool_env('ML_BLOCK_LOCAL_IP', False)
limitations_or_blockers: Docker image pull timed out in the execution environment due to network constraints
Runtime verification could not be completed

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag 1.23.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
